prompt: Se fossi uno user che installa questa skill per la prima volta, cosa ti confonderebbe nei primi 5 minuti?
content:
Confusion points, first 5 minutes:

**Name collision — worst offender.** 4 things named `flint`:
- `/flint` slash skill (one-shot)
- `flint-on` / `flint-off` slash skills (persistent)
- `flint` shell binary (wrapper around `claude`)
- `flint` output-style (set via `/config`)
- plus `flint-ir` Python CLI, plus `flint-mcp` wrapper

User types `flint` → which one fires? README calls all "flint". Memory note already flags this (`flint` vs `flint-ir` conflated).

**"Which persistent path do I pick?"** Three options, all equivalent-sounding:
- `/flint-on` (per-conversation)
- output-style `flint` via `/config` (per-session)
- `flint` binary (always-on, Claude Code Max only)

Line 34 explains why binary > output-style, but buried. User picks wrong one, sees no compression, blames tool.

**Silent PATH miss.** Install prints `~/.local/bin` warning. User scrolls past. Types `flint` → "command not found". No follow-up check.

**Python package "optional" — but `/flint-audit` needs it.** Installer says "optional". `flint-audit` skill shells out to `flint-ir audit --explain`. pipx/pip missing → skill silently broken on first use.

**`mcp` package not auto-installed.** `flint-mcp` documented early, feels first-class. Running it → ImportError. Hint at end of install output, easy to miss.

**Drift-fix hook only active via `flint` binary.** Settings file dropped at `~/.claude/flint-drift-fix-settings.json` but not merged into user `settings.json`. `/flint-on` in plain `claude` → no drift repair. Nowhere stated plainly.

**`claude` untouched — then how use?** Reassurance ("default `claude` never touched") good for safety, confusing for onboarding. User wonders: do I type `claude` or `flint`?

**`/flint` vs `flint-mcp` decision.** Benchmark tables (classification, IR_hit, tool_hit, MCP overhead) must be read to choose. No "start here" TL;DR.

**Install output wall.** 30 lines of bullets at end — slash commands, output-styles, binaries, MCP variant. No "try this first" line.

Fix suggestion: 3-line post-install message — "Try `/flint ping` now. For always-on: `flint`. Everything else: `README.md`."

input.command: for s in flint flint-on flint-off flint-audit; do echo "=== $s ==="; head -20 /home/tommaso/dev/playground/SIGIL/integrations/claude-code/skills/$s/SKILL.md; done
